digest.name: digest

features.description: Allow containers access to advanced features.

force_rw_sys.description: Mount /sys in unprivileged containers as `rw` instead of `mixed`. This can break networking under newer (>= v245) systemd-network use.

fuse.description: Allow using 'fuse' file systems in a container. Note that interactions between fuse and the freezer cgroup can potentially cause I/O deadlocks.

keyctl.description: For unprivileged containers only: Allow the use of the keyctl() system call. This is required to use docker inside a container. By default unprivileged containers will see this system call as non-existent. This is mostly a workaround for systemd-networkd, as it will treat it as a fatal error when some keyctl() operations are denied by the kernel due to lacking permissions. Essentially, you can choose between running systemd-networkd or docker.

mknod.description: Allow unprivileged containers to use mknod() to add certain device nodes. This requires a kernel with seccomp trap to user space support (5.3 or newer). This is experimental.

mount.description: Allow mounting file systems of specific types. This should be a list of file system types as used with the mount command. Note that this can have negative effects on the container's security. With access to a loop device, mounting a file can circumvent the mknod permission of the devices cgroup, mounting an NFS file system can block the host's I/O completely and prevent it from rebooting, etc.

nesting.description: Allow nesting. Best used with unprivileged containers with additional id mapping. Note that this will expose procfs and sysfs contents of the host to the guest.

features.name: features

hookscript.description: Script that will be executed during various steps in the containers lifetime.
hookscript.name: hookscript

hostname.description: Set a host name for the container.
hostname.name: hostname

memory.description: Amount of RAM for the container in MB.
memory.name: memory

mp[n].description: Use volume as container mount point. Use the special syntax STORAGE_ID:SIZE_IN_GiB to allocate a new volume.

acl.description: Explicitly enable or disable ACL support.

backup.description: Whether to include the mount point in backups.
backup.verbose_description: Whether to include the mount point in backups (only used for volume mount points).

mp.description: Path to the mount point as seen from inside the container (must not contain symlinks).
mp.verbose_description:
Path to the mount point as seen from inside the container.

NOTE: Must not contain any symlinks for security reasons.

quota.description: Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs subvolumes)

replicate.description: Will include this volume to a storage replica job.

shared.description: Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see 'nodes')
shared.verbose_description:
Mark this non-volume mount point as available on all nodes.

WARNING: This option does not share the mount point automatically, it assumes it is shared already!

size.description: Volume size (read only value).

volume.description: Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.

mp[n].name: mp[n]

nameserver.description: Sets DNS server IP address for a container. Create will automatically use the setting from the host if you neither set searchdomain nor nameserver.
nameserver.name: nameserver

net[n].description: Specifies network interfaces for the container.

bridge.description: Bridge to attach the network device to.

firewall.description: Controls whether this interface's firewall rules should be used.

hwaddr.description: The interface MAC address. This is dynamically allocated by default, but you can set that statically if needed, for example to always have the same link-local IPv6 address. (lxc.network.hwaddr)
hwaddr.verbose_description: A common MAC address with the I/G (Individual/Group) bit not set.

link_down.description: Whether this interface should be disconnected (like pulling the plug).

mtu.description: Maximum transfer unit of the interface. (lxc.network.mtu)

name.description: Name of the network device as seen from inside the container. (lxc.network.name)

rate.description: Apply rate limiting to the interface

trunks.description: VLAN ids to pass through the interface

net[n].name: net[n]

onboot.description: Specifies whether a container will be started during system bootup.
onboot.name: onboot

ostype.description: OS type. This is used to setup configuration inside the container, and corresponds to lxc setup scripts in /usr/share/lxc/config/<ostype>.common.conf. Value 'unmanaged' can be used to skip and OS specific setup.
ostype.name: ostype

protection.description: Sets the protection flag of the container. This will prevent the CT or CT's disk remove/update operation.
protection.name: protection

acl.description: Explicitly enable or disable ACL support.

quota.description: Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs subvolumes)

replicate.description: Will include this volume to a storage replica job.

shared.description: Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see 'nodes')
shared.verbose_description:
Mark this non-volume mount point as available on all nodes.

WARNING: This option does not share the mount point automatically, it assumes it is shared already!

size.description: Volume size (read only value).

volume.description: Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.

searchdomain.description: Sets DNS search domains for a container. Create will automatically use the setting from the host if you neither set searchdomain nor nameserver.
searchdomain.name: searchdomain

startup.description: Startup and shutdown behavior. Order is a non-negative number defining the general startup order. Shutdown in done with reverse ordering. Additionally you can set the 'up' or 'down' delay in seconds, which specifies a delay to wait before the next VM is started or stopped.
startup.name: startup

swap.description: Amount of SWAP for the container in MB.
swap.name: swap

tags.description: Tags of the Container. This is only meta information.
tags.name: tags

timezone.description: Time zone to use in the container. If option isn't set, then nothing will be done. Can be set to 'host' to match the host time zone, or an arbitrary time zone option from /usr/share/zoneinfo/zone.tab
timezone.name: timezone

tty.description: Specify the number of tty available to the container
tty.name: tty

unprivileged.description: Makes the container run as unprivileged user. (Should not be modified manually.)
unprivileged.name: unprivileged

unused[n].description: Reference to unused volumes. This is used internally, and should not be modified manually.

volume.description: The volume that is not used currently.

unused[n].name: unused[n]

permissions.description: non-volume mount points in rootfs and mp[n] are restricted to root@pam

raw_sections:
properties:{
    "arch": {
        "default": "amd64",
        "description": "OS architecture type.",
        "enum": [
            "amd64",
            "i386",
            "arm64",
            "armhf",
            "riscv32",
            "riscv64"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "arch",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-361"
    },
    "cmode": {
        "default": "tty",
        "description": "Console mode. By default, the console command tries to open a connection to one of the available tty devices. By setting cmode to 'console' it tries to attach to /dev/console instead. If you set cmode to 'shell', it simply invokes a shell inside the container (no login).",
        "enum": [
            "shell",
            "console",
            "tty"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "cmode",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-362"
    },
    "console": {
        "default": 1,
        "description": "Attach a console device (/dev/console) to the container.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "console",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-363"
    },
    "cores": {
        "description": "The number of cores assigned to the container. A container can use all available cores by default.",
        "maximum": 8192,
        "minimum": 1,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "cores",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-364"
    },
    "cpulimit": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Limit of CPU usage.\n\nNOTE: If the computer has 2 CPUs, it has a total of '2' CPU time. Value '0' indicates no CPU limit.",
        "maximum": 8192,
        "minimum": 0,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "number",
        "name": "cpulimit",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-365"
    },
    "cpuunits": {
        "default": "cgroup v1: 1024, cgroup v2: 100",
        "description": "CPU weight for a container, will be clamped to [1, 10000] in cgroup v2.",
        "maximum": 500000,
        "minimum": 0,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "integer",
        "verbose_description": "CPU weight for a container. Argument is used in the kernel fair scheduler. The larger the number is, the more CPU time this container gets. Number is relative to the weights of all the other running guests.",
        "name": "cpuunits",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-366"
    },
    "debug": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Try to be more verbose. For now this only enables debug log-level on start.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "debug",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-367"
    },
    "description": {
        "description": "Description for the Container. Shown in the web-interface CT's summary. This is saved as comment inside the configuration file.",
        "maxLength": 8192,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "description",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-368"
    },
    "dev[n]": {
        "description": "Device to pass through to the container",
        "format": {
            "deny-write": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "Deny the container to write to the device",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "gid": {
                "description": "Group ID to be assigned to the device node",
                "minimum": 0,
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "integer"
            },
            "mode": {
                "description": "Access mode to be set on the device node",
                "format_description": "Octal access mode",
                "optional": 1,
                "pattern": "0[0-7]{3}",
                "type": "string"
            },
            "path": {
                "default_key": 1,
                "description": "Device to pass through to the container",
                "format": "pve-lxc-dev-string",
                "format_description": "Path",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string",
                "verbose_description": "Path to the device to pass through to the container"
            },
            "uid": {
                "description": "User ID to be assigned to the device node",
                "minimum": 0,
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "integer"
            }
        },
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "dev[n]",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-369"
    },
    "digest": {
        "description": "SHA1 digest of configuration file. This can be used to prevent concurrent modifications.",
        "type": "string",
        "name": "digest",
        "optional": false,
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-370"
    },
    "features": {
        "description": "Allow containers access to advanced features.",
        "format": {
            "force_rw_sys": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "Mount /sys in unprivileged containers as `rw` instead of `mixed`. This can break networking under newer (>= v245) systemd-network use.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "fuse": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "Allow using 'fuse' file systems in a container. Note that interactions between fuse and the freezer cgroup can potentially cause I/O deadlocks.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "keyctl": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "For unprivileged containers only: Allow the use of the keyctl() system call. This is required to use docker inside a container. By default unprivileged containers will see this system call as non-existent. This is mostly a workaround for systemd-networkd, as it will treat it as a fatal error when some keyctl() operations are denied by the kernel due to lacking permissions. Essentially, you can choose between running systemd-networkd or docker.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "mknod": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "Allow unprivileged containers to use mknod() to add certain device nodes. This requires a kernel with seccomp trap to user space support (5.3 or newer). This is experimental.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "mount": {
                "description": "Allow mounting file systems of specific types. This should be a list of file system types as used with the mount command. Note that this can have negative effects on the container's security. With access to a loop device, mounting a file can circumvent the mknod permission of the devices cgroup, mounting an NFS file system can block the host's I/O completely and prevent it from rebooting, etc.",
                "format_description": "fstype;fstype;...",
                "optional": 1,
                "pattern": "(?^:[a-zA-Z0-9_; ]+)",
                "type": "string"
            },
            "nesting": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "Allow nesting. Best used with unprivileged containers with additional id mapping. Note that this will expose procfs and sysfs contents of the host to the guest.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            }
        },
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "features",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-371"
    },
    "hookscript": {
        "description": "Script that will be executed during various steps in the containers lifetime.",
        "format": "pve-volume-id",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "hookscript",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-372"
    },
    "hostname": {
        "description": "Set a host name for the container.",
        "format": "dns-name",
        "maxLength": 255,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "hostname",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-373"
    },
    "lock": {
        "description": "Lock/unlock the container.",
        "enum": [
            "backup",
            "create",
            "destroyed",
            "disk",
            "fstrim",
            "migrate",
            "mounted",
            "rollback",
            "snapshot",
            "snapshot-delete"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "lock",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-374"
    },
    "lxc": {
        "description": "Array of lxc low-level configurations ([[key1, value1], [key2, value2] ...]).",
        "items": {
            "items": {
                "type": "string"
            },
            "type": "array"
        },
        "optional": true,
        "type": "array",
        "name": "lxc",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-375"
    },
    "memory": {
        "default": 512,
        "description": "Amount of RAM for the container in MB.",
        "minimum": 16,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "memory",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-376"
    },
    "mp[n]": {
        "description": "Use volume as container mount point. Use the special syntax STORAGE_ID:SIZE_IN_GiB to allocate a new volume.",
        "format": {
            "acl": {
                "description": "Explicitly enable or disable ACL support.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "backup": {
                "description": "Whether to include the mount point in backups.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean",
                "verbose_description": "Whether to include the mount point in backups (only used for volume mount points)."
            },
            "mountoptions": {
                "description": "Extra mount options for rootfs/mps.",
                "format_description": "opt[;opt...]",
                "optional": 1,
                "pattern": "(?^:(?^:(discard|lazytime|noatime|nodev|noexec|nosuid))(;(?^:(discard|lazytime|noatime|nodev|noexec|nosuid)))*)",
                "type": "string"
            },
            "mp": {
                "description": "Path to the mount point as seen from inside the container (must not contain symlinks).",
                "format": "pve-lxc-mp-string",
                "format_description": "Path",
                "type": "string",
                "verbose_description": "Path to the mount point as seen from inside the container.\n\nNOTE: Must not contain any symlinks for security reasons."
            },
            "quota": {
                "description": "Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs subvolumes)",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "replicate": {
                "default": 1,
                "description": "Will include this volume to a storage replica job.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "ro": {
                "description": "Read-only mount point",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "shared": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see 'nodes')",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean",
                "verbose_description": "Mark this non-volume mount point as available on all nodes.\n\nWARNING: This option does not share the mount point automatically, it assumes it is shared already!"
            },
            "size": {
                "description": "Volume size (read only value).",
                "format": "disk-size",
                "format_description": "DiskSize",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string"
            },
            "volume": {
                "default_key": 1,
                "description": "Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.",
                "format": "pve-lxc-mp-string",
                "format_description": "volume",
                "type": "string"
            }
        },
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "mp[n]",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-377"
    },
    "nameserver": {
        "description": "Sets DNS server IP address for a container. Create will automatically use the setting from the host if you neither set searchdomain nor nameserver.",
        "format": "lxc-ip-with-ll-iface-list",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "nameserver",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-378"
    },
    "net[n]": {
        "description": "Specifies network interfaces for the container.",
        "format": {
            "bridge": {
                "description": "Bridge to attach the network device to.",
                "format_description": "bridge",
                "optional": 1,
                "pattern": "[-_.\\w\\d]+",
                "type": "string"
            },
            "firewall": {
                "description": "Controls whether this interface's firewall rules should be used.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "gw": {
                "description": "Default gateway for IPv4 traffic.",
                "format": "ipv4",
                "format_description": "GatewayIPv4",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string"
            },
            "gw6": {
                "description": "Default gateway for IPv6 traffic.",
                "format": "ipv6",
                "format_description": "GatewayIPv6",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string"
            },
            "hwaddr": {
                "description": "The interface MAC address. This is dynamically allocated by default, but you can set that statically if needed, for example to always have the same link-local IPv6 address. (lxc.network.hwaddr)",
                "format": "mac-addr",
                "format_description": "XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string",
                "verbose_description": "A common MAC address with the I/G (Individual/Group) bit not set."
            },
            "ip": {
                "description": "IPv4 address in CIDR format.",
                "format": "pve-ipv4-config",
                "format_description": "(IPv4/CIDR|dhcp|manual)",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string"
            },
            "ip6": {
                "description": "IPv6 address in CIDR format.",
                "format": "pve-ipv6-config",
                "format_description": "(IPv6/CIDR|auto|dhcp|manual)",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string"
            },
            "link_down": {
                "description": "Whether this interface should be disconnected (like pulling the plug).",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "mtu": {
                "description": "Maximum transfer unit of the interface. (lxc.network.mtu)",
                "maximum": 65535,
                "minimum": 64,
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "integer"
            },
            "name": {
                "description": "Name of the network device as seen from inside the container. (lxc.network.name)",
                "format_description": "string",
                "pattern": "[-_.\\w\\d]+",
                "type": "string"
            },
            "rate": {
                "description": "Apply rate limiting to the interface",
                "format_description": "mbps",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "number"
            },
            "tag": {
                "description": "VLAN tag for this interface.",
                "maximum": 4094,
                "minimum": 1,
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "integer"
            },
            "trunks": {
                "description": "VLAN ids to pass through the interface",
                "format_description": "vlanid[;vlanid...]",
                "optional": 1,
                "pattern": "(?^:\\d+(?:;\\d+)*)",
                "type": "string"
            },
            "type": {
                "description": "Network interface type.",
                "enum": [
                    "veth"
                ],
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string"
            }
        },
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "net[n]",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-379"
    },
    "onboot": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Specifies whether a container will be started during system bootup.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "onboot",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-380"
    },
    "ostype": {
        "description": "OS type. This is used to setup configuration inside the container, and corresponds to lxc setup scripts in /usr/share/lxc/config/<ostype>.common.conf. Value 'unmanaged' can be used to skip and OS specific setup.",
        "enum": [
            "debian",
            "devuan",
            "ubuntu",
            "centos",
            "fedora",
            "opensuse",
            "archlinux",
            "alpine",
            "gentoo",
            "nixos",
            "unmanaged"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "ostype",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-381"
    },
    "protection": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Sets the protection flag of the container. This will prevent the CT or CT's disk remove/update operation.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "protection",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-382"
    },
    "rootfs": {
        "description": "Use volume as container root.",
        "format": {
            "acl": {
                "description": "Explicitly enable or disable ACL support.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "mountoptions": {
                "description": "Extra mount options for rootfs/mps.",
                "format_description": "opt[;opt...]",
                "optional": 1,
                "pattern": "(?^:(?^:(discard|lazytime|noatime|nodev|noexec|nosuid))(;(?^:(discard|lazytime|noatime|nodev|noexec|nosuid)))*)",
                "type": "string"
            },
            "quota": {
                "description": "Enable user quotas inside the container (not supported with zfs subvolumes)",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "replicate": {
                "default": 1,
                "description": "Will include this volume to a storage replica job.",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "ro": {
                "description": "Read-only mount point",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean"
            },
            "shared": {
                "default": 0,
                "description": "Mark this non-volume mount point as available on multiple nodes (see 'nodes')",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "boolean",
                "verbose_description": "Mark this non-volume mount point as available on all nodes.\n\nWARNING: This option does not share the mount point automatically, it assumes it is shared already!"
            },
            "size": {
                "description": "Volume size (read only value).",
                "format": "disk-size",
                "format_description": "DiskSize",
                "optional": 1,
                "type": "string"
            },
            "volume": {
                "default_key": 1,
                "description": "Volume, device or directory to mount into the container.",
                "format": "pve-lxc-mp-string",
                "format_description": "volume",
                "type": "string"
            }
        },
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "rootfs",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-383"
    },
    "searchdomain": {
        "description": "Sets DNS search domains for a container. Create will automatically use the setting from the host if you neither set searchdomain nor nameserver.",
        "format": "dns-name-list",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "searchdomain",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-384"
    },
    "startup": {
        "description": "Startup and shutdown behavior. Order is a non-negative number defining the general startup order. Shutdown in done with reverse ordering. Additionally you can set the 'up' or 'down' delay in seconds, which specifies a delay to wait before the next VM is started or stopped.",
        "format": "pve-startup-order",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "typetext": "[[order=]\\d+] [,up=\\d+] [,down=\\d+] ",
        "name": "startup",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-385"
    },
    "swap": {
        "default": 512,
        "description": "Amount of SWAP for the container in MB.",
        "minimum": 0,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "swap",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-386"
    },
    "tags": {
        "description": "Tags of the Container. This is only meta information.",
        "format": "pve-tag-list",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "tags",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-387"
    },
    "template": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Enable/disable Template.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "template",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-388"
    },
    "timezone": {
        "description": "Time zone to use in the container. If option isn't set, then nothing will be done. Can be set to 'host' to match the host time zone, or an arbitrary time zone option from /usr/share/zoneinfo/zone.tab",
        "format": "pve-ct-timezone",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "timezone",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-389"
    },
    "tty": {
        "default": 2,
        "description": "Specify the number of tty available to the container",
        "maximum": 6,
        "minimum": 0,
        "optional": true,
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "tty",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-390"
    },
    "unprivileged": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Makes the container run as unprivileged user. (Should not be modified manually.)",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "unprivileged",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-391"
    },
    "unused[n]": {
        "description": "Reference to unused volumes. This is used internally, and should not be modified manually.",
        "format": {
            "volume": {
                "default_key": 1,
                "description": "The volume that is not used currently.",
                "format": "pve-volume-id",
                "format_description": "volume",
                "type": "string"
            }
        },
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "unused[n]",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-392"
    }
}

properties:{
    "dhcp": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Enable DHCP.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "dhcp",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-346"
    },
    "enable": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Enable/disable firewall rules.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "enable",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-347"
    },
    "ipfilter": {
        "description": "Enable default IP filters. This is equivalent to adding an empty ipfilter-net<id> ipset for every interface. Such ipsets implicitly contain sane default restrictions such as restricting IPv6 link local addresses to the one derived from the interface's MAC address. For containers the configured IP addresses will be implicitly added.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "ipfilter",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-348"
    },
    "log_level_in": {
        "description": "Log level for incoming traffic.",
        "enum": [
            "emerg",
            "alert",
            "crit",
            "err",
            "warning",
            "notice",
            "info",
            "debug",
            "nolog"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "log_level_in",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-349"
    },
    "log_level_out": {
        "description": "Log level for outgoing traffic.",
        "enum": [
            "emerg",
            "alert",
            "crit",
            "err",
            "warning",
            "notice",
            "info",
            "debug",
            "nolog"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "log_level_out",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-350"
    },
    "macfilter": {
        "default": 1,
        "description": "Enable/disable MAC address filter.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "macfilter",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-351"
    },
    "ndp": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Enable NDP (Neighbor Discovery Protocol).",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "ndp",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-352"
    },
    "policy_in": {
        "description": "Input policy.",
        "enum": [
            "ACCEPT",
            "REJECT",
            "DROP"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "policy_in",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-353"
    },
    "policy_out": {
        "description": "Output policy.",
        "enum": [
            "ACCEPT",
            "REJECT",
            "DROP"
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "policy_out",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-354"
    },
    "radv": {
        "description": "Allow sending Router Advertisement.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "radv",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-355"
    }
}

GET.description: Lists possible IPSet/Alias reference which are allowed in source/dest properties.

type.description: Only list references of specified type.
type.name: type

action.description: Rule action ('ACCEPT', 'DROP', 'REJECT') or security group name.
action.name: action

dest.description: Restrict packet destination address. This can refer to a single IP address, an IP set ('+ipsetname') or an IP alias definition. You can also specify an address range like '20.34.101.207-201.3.9.99', or a list of IP addresses and networks (entries are separated by comma). Please do not mix IPv4 and IPv6 addresses inside such lists.
dest.name: dest

digest.description: Prevent changes if current configuration file has a different digest. This can be used to prevent concurrent modifications.
digest.name: digest

dport.description: Restrict TCP/UDP destination port. You can use service names or simple numbers (0-65535), as defined in '/etc/services'. Port ranges can be specified with '\d+:\d+', for example '80:85', and you can use comma separated list to match several ports or ranges.
dport.name: dport

icmp-type.description: Specify icmp-type. Only valid if proto equals 'icmp' or 'icmpv6'/'ipv6-icmp'.
icmp-type.name: icmp-type

iface.description: Network interface name. You have to use network configuration key names for VMs and containers ('net\d+'). Host related rules can use arbitrary strings.
iface.name: iface

proto.description: IP protocol. You can use protocol names ('tcp'/'udp') or simple numbers, as defined in '/etc/protocols'.
proto.name: proto

source.description: Restrict packet source address. This can refer to a single IP address, an IP set ('+ipsetname') or an IP alias definition. You can also specify an address range like '20.34.101.207-201.3.9.99', or a list of IP addresses and networks (entries are separated by comma). Please do not mix IPv4 and IPv6 addresses inside such lists.
source.name: source

sport.description: Restrict TCP/UDP source port. You can use service names or simple numbers (0-65535), as defined in '/etc/services'. Port ranges can be specified with '\d+:\d+', for example '80:85', and you can use comma separated list to match several ports or ranges.
sport.name: sport

digest.name: digest

action.name: action

Please do not mix IPv4 and IPv6 addresses inside such lists.

hwaddr.description: The MAC address of the interface
hwaddr.name: hwaddr

inet.description: The IPv4 address of the interface
inet.name: inet

inet6.description: The IPv6 address of the interface
inet6.name: inet6

POST.description: Migrate the container to another node. Creates a new migration task.

bwlimit.default: migrate limit from datacenter or storage config
bwlimit.name: bwlimit

target-storage.description: Mapping from source to target storages. Providing only a single storage ID maps all source storages to that storage. Providing the special value '1' will map each source storage to itself.
target-storage.name: target-storage

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for shutdown for restart migration
timeout.name: timeout

returns.description: the task ID.

POST.description: Move a rootfs-/mp-volume to a different storage or to a different container.

bwlimit.default: clone limit from datacenter or storage config
bwlimit.name: bwlimit

delete.description: Delete the original volume after successful copy. By default the original is kept as an unused volume entry.
delete.name: delete

digest.description:
Prevent changes if current configuration file has different SHA1 " .
		    "digest. This can be used to prevent concurrent modifications.
digest.name: digest

target-digest.description:
Prevent changes if current configuration file of the target " .
		    "container has a different SHA1 digest. This can be used to prevent " .
		    "concurrent modifications.
target-digest.name: target-digest

target-volume.description: The config key the volume will be moved to. Default is the source volume key.
target-volume.name: target-volume

permissions.description: You need 'VM.Config.Disk' permissions on /vms/{vmid}, and 'Datastore.AllocateSpace' permissions on the storage. To move a volume to another container, you need the permissions on the target container as well.

POST.description: Migration tunnel endpoint - only for internal use by CT migration.

bridges.description: List of network bridges to check availability. Will be checked again for actually used bridges during migration.
bridges.name: bridges

storages.description: List of storages to check permission and availability. Will be checked again for all actually used storages during migration.
storages.name: storages

permissions.description: You need 'VM.Allocate' permissions on '/vms/{vmid}' and Sys.Incoming on '/'. Further permission checks happen during the actual migration.

GET.description: Migration tunnel endpoint for websocket upgrade - only for internal use by VM migration.

ticket.description: ticket return by initial 'mtunnel' API call, or retrieved via 'ticket' tunnel command
ticket.name: ticket

permissions.description: You need to pass a ticket valid for the selected socket. Tickets can be created via the mtunnel API call, which will check permissions accordingly.

GET.description: Get container configuration, including pending changes.

delete.description: Indicates a pending delete request if present and not 0.
delete.name: delete

POST.description: Migrate the container to another cluster. Creates a new migration task. EXPERIMENTAL feature!

bwlimit.default: migrate limit from datacenter or storage config
bwlimit.name: bwlimit

delete.description: Delete the original CT and related data after successful migration. By default the original CT is kept on the source cluster in a stopped state.
delete.name: delete

target-bridge.description: Mapping from source to target bridges. Providing only a single bridge ID maps all source bridges to that bridge. Providing the special value '1' will map each source bridge to itself.
target-bridge.name: target-bridge

target-storage.description: Mapping from source to target storages. Providing only a single storage ID maps all source storages to that storage. Providing the special value '1' will map each source storage to itself.
target-storage.name: target-storage

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for shutdown for restart migration
timeout.name: timeout

returns.description: the task ID.

digest.description: Prevent changes if current configuration file has different SHA1 digest. This can be used to prevent concurrent modifications.
digest.name: digest

disk.description: The disk you want to resize.
disk.name: disk

size.description: The new size. With the '+' sign the value is added to the actual size of the volume and without it, the value is taken as an absolute one. Shrinking disk size is not supported.
size.name: size

returns.description: the task ID.

ds.description: The list of datasources you want to display.
ds.name: ds

timeframe.description: Specify the time frame you are interested in.
timeframe.name: timeframe

timeframe.description: Specify the time frame you are interested in.
timeframe.name: timeframe

description.description: Snapshot description.
description.name: description

name.description: Snapshot identifier. Value 'current' identifies the current VM.
name.name: name

description.description: A textual description or comment.
description.name: description

returns.description: the task ID.

force.description: For removal from config file, even if removing disk snapshots fails.
force.name: force

returns.description: the task ID.

description.description: A textual description or comment.
description.name: description

POST.description: Rollback LXC state to specified snapshot.

start.description: Whether the container should get started after rolling back successfully
start.name: start

returns.description: the task ID.

POST.description: Returns a SPICE configuration to connect to the CT.

proxy.description: SPICE proxy server. This can be used by the client to specify the proxy server. All nodes in a cluster runs 'spiceproxy', so it is up to the client to choose one. By default, we return the node where the VM is currently running. As reasonable setting is to use same node you use to connect to the API (This is window.location.hostname for the JS GUI).
proxy.name: proxy

returns.description: Returned values can be directly passed to the 'remote-viewer' application.

raw_sections:
properties:{
    "cpus": {
        "description": "Maximum usable CPUs.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "number",
        "name": "cpus",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-334"
    },
    "disk": {
        "description": "Root disk image space-usage in bytes.",
        "minimum": 0,
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "disk",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-335"
    },
    "diskread": {
        "description": "The amount of bytes the guest read from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "diskread",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-336"
    },
    "diskwrite": {
        "description": "The amount of bytes the guest wrote from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "diskwrite",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-337"
    },
    "ha": {
        "description": "HA manager service status.",
        "type": "object",
        "name": "ha",
        "optional": false,
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-338"
    },
    "lock": {
        "description": "The current config lock, if any.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "lock",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-339"
    },
    "maxdisk": {
        "description": "Root disk image size in bytes.",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "maxdisk",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-340"
    },
    "maxmem": {
        "description": "Maximum memory in bytes.",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "maxmem",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-341"
    },
    "maxswap": {
        "description": "Maximum SWAP memory in bytes.",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "maxswap",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-342"
    },
    "name": {
        "description": "Container name.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "name",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-343"
    },
    "netin": {
        "description": "The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent to the guest over the network since it was started.",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "netin",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-344"
    },
    "netout": {
        "description": "The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent from the guest over the network since it was started.",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "bytes",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "netout",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-345"
    },
    "status": {
        "description": "LXC Container status.",
        "enum": [
            "stopped",
            "running"
        ],
        "type": "string",
        "name": "status",
        "optional": false,
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-346"
    },
    "tags": {
        "description": "The current configured tags, if any.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "string",
        "name": "tags",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-347"
    },
    "template": {
        "default": 0,
        "description": "Determines if the guest is a template.",
        "optional": true,
        "type": "boolean",
        "name": "template",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-348"
    },
    "uptime": {
        "description": "Uptime in seconds.",
        "optional": true,
        "renderer": "duration",
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "uptime",
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-349"
    },
    "vmid": {
        "description": "The (unique) ID of the VM.",
        "format": "pve-vmid",
        "maximum": 999999999,
        "minimum": 100,
        "type": "integer",
        "name": "vmid",
        "optional": false,
        "id": "pmx-param-schema-350"
    }
}

disk.description: Root disk image space-usage in bytes.
disk.name: disk

diskread.description: The amount of bytes the guest read from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)
diskread.name: diskread

diskwrite.description: The amount of bytes the guest wrote from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)
diskwrite.name: diskwrite

lock.description: The current config lock, if any.
lock.name: lock

maxdisk.description: Root disk image size in bytes.
maxdisk.name: maxdisk

netin.description: The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent to the guest over the network since it was started.
netin.name: netin

netout.description: The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent from the guest over the network since it was started.
netout.name: netout

tags.description: The current configured tags, if any.
tags.name: tags

template.description: Determines if the guest is a template.
template.name: template

POST.description: Reboot the container by shutting it down, and starting it again. Applies pending changes.

timeout.description: Wait maximal timeout seconds for the shutdown.
timeout.name: timeout

POST.description: Shutdown the container. This will trigger a clean shutdown of the container, see lxc-stop(1) for details.

debug.description: If set, enables very verbose debug log-level on start.
debug.name: debug

skiplock.description: Ignore locks - only root is allowed to use this option.
skiplock.name: skiplock

POST.description: Stop the container. This will abruptly stop all processes running in the container.

overrule-shutdown.description: Try to abort active 'vzshutdown' tasks before stopping.
overrule-shutdown.name: overrule-shutdown

skiplock.description: Ignore locks - only root is allowed to use this option.
skiplock.name: skiplock

POST.description: Suspend the container. This is experimental.

permissions.description: You need 'VM.Allocate' permissions on /vms/{vmid}

POST.description: Creates a TCP VNC proxy connections.

height.description: sets the height of the console in pixels.
height.name: height

websocket.description: use websocket instead of standard VNC.
websocket.name: websocket

width.description: sets the width of the console in pixels.
width.name: width

GET.description: Opens a weksocket for VNC traffic.

port.description: Port number returned by previous vncproxy call.
port.name: port

vncticket.description: Ticket from previous call to vncproxy.
vncticket.name: vncticket

permissions.description: You also need to pass a valid ticket (vncticket).

maxworkers.description: Maximal number of parallel migration job. If not set, uses'max_workers' from datacenter.cfg. One of both must be set!
maxworkers.name: maxworkers

vms.description: Only consider Guests with these IDs.
vms.name: vms

with-local-disks.description: Enable live storage migration for local disk
with-local-disks.name: with-local-disks

permissions.description: The 'VM.Migrate' permission is required on '/' or on '/vms/<ID>' for each ID passed via the 'vms' parameter.

GET.description: Read tap/vm network device interface counters

bond-primary.description: Specify the primary interface for active-backup bond.
bond-primary.name: bond-primary

bond_xmit_hash_policy.description: Selects the transmit hash policy to use for slave selection in balance-xor and 802.3ad modes.

raw_sections:
items: {
    "properties": {
        "cpus": {
            "description": "Maximum usable CPUs.",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "number",
            "name": "cpus",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-388"
        },
        "diskread": {
            "description": "The amount of bytes the guest read from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)",
            "optional": true,
            "renderer": "bytes",
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "diskread",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-389"
        },
        "diskwrite": {
            "description": "The amount of bytes the guest wrote from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)",
            "optional": true,
            "renderer": "bytes",
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "diskwrite",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-390"
        },
        "lock": {
            "description": "The current config lock, if any.",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "string",
            "name": "lock",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-391"
        },
        "maxdisk": {
            "description": "Root disk size in bytes.",
            "optional": true,
            "renderer": "bytes",
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "maxdisk",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-392"
        },
        "maxmem": {
            "description": "Maximum memory in bytes.",
            "optional": true,
            "renderer": "bytes",
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "maxmem",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-393"
        },
        "name": {
            "description": "VM (host)name.",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "string",
            "name": "name",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-394"
        },
        "netin": {
            "description": "The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent to the guest over the network since it was started.",
            "optional": true,
            "renderer": "bytes",
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "netin",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-395"
        },
        "netout": {
            "description": "The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent from the guest over the network since it was started.",
            "optional": true,
            "renderer": "bytes",
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "netout",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-396"
        },
        "pid": {
            "description": "PID of the QEMU process, if the VM is running.",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "pid",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-397"
        },
        "qmpstatus": {
            "description": "VM run state from the 'query-status' QMP monitor command.",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "string",
            "name": "qmpstatus",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-398"
        },
        "running-machine": {
            "description": "The currently running machine type (if running).",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "string",
            "name": "running-machine",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-399"
        },
        "running-qemu": {
            "description": "The QEMU version the VM is currently using (if running).",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "string",
            "name": "running-qemu",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-400"
        },
        "status": {
            "description": "QEMU process status.",
            "enum": [
                "stopped",
                "running"
            ],
            "type": "string",
            "name": "status",
            "optional": false,
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-401"
        },
        "tags": {
            "description": "The current configured tags, if any",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "string",
            "name": "tags",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-402"
        },
        "template": {
            "default": 0,
            "description": "Determines if the guest is a template.",
            "optional": true,
            "type": "boolean",
            "name": "template",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-403"
        },
        "uptime": {
            "description": "Uptime in seconds.",
            "optional": true,
            "renderer": "duration",
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "uptime",
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-404"
        },
        "vmid": {
            "description": "The (unique) ID of the VM.",
            "format": "pve-vmid",
            "maximum": 999999999,
            "minimum": 100,
            "type": "integer",
            "name": "vmid",
            "optional": false,
            "id": "pmx-param-schema-405"
        }
    },
    "type": "object"
}

diskread.description: The amount of bytes the guest read from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)
diskread.name: diskread

diskwrite.description: The amount of bytes the guest wrote from it's block devices since the guest was started. (Note: This info is not available for all storage types.)
diskwrite.name: diskwrite

lock.description: The current config lock, if any.
lock.name: lock

netin.description: The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent to the guest over the network since it was started.
netin.name: netin

netout.description: The amount of traffic in bytes that was sent from the guest over the network since it was started.
netout.name: netout

pid.description: PID of the QEMU process, if the VM is running.
pid.name: pid

qmpstatus.description: VM run state from the 'query-status' QMP monitor command.
qmpstatus.name: qmpstatus

running-machine.description: The currently running machine type (if running).
running-machine.name: running-machine

running-qemu.description: The QEMU version the VM is currently using (if running).
running-qemu.name: running-qemu

tags.description: The current configured tags, if any
tags.name: tags

template.description: Determines if the guest is a template.
template.name: template

POST.description: Create or restore a virtual machine.

affinity.description: List of host cores used to execute guest processes, for example: 0,5,8-11
affinity.name: affinity

agent.description: Enable/disable communication with the QEMU Guest Agent and its properties.

enabled.description: Enable/disable communication with a QEMU Guest Agent (QGA) running in the VM.

freeze-fs-on-backup.description: Freeze/thaw guest filesystems on backup for consistency.

fstrim_cloned_disks.description: Run fstrim after moving a disk or migrating the VM.

agent.name: agent

amd-sev.description: Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) features by AMD CPUs
amd-sev.name: amd-sev

arch.description: Virtual processor architecture. Defaults to the host.
arch.name: arch

archive.description: The backup archive. Either the file system path to a .tar or .vma file (use '-' to pipe data from stdin) or a proxmox storage backup volume identifier.
archive.name: archive

args.name: args
args.verbose_description:
Arbitrary arguments passed to kvm, for example:

args: -no-reboot -smbios 'type=0,vendor=FOO'

NOTE: this option is for experts only.

audio0.description: Configure a audio device, useful in combination with QXL/Spice.

driver.description: Driver backend for the audio device.

audio0.name: audio0

balloon.description: Amount of target RAM for the VM in MiB. Using zero disables the ballon driver.
balloon.name: balloon

boot.description: Specify guest boot order. Use the 'order=' sub-property as usage with no key or 'legacy=' is deprecated.
boot.name: boot

bootdisk.description: Enable booting from specified disk. Deprecated: Use 'boot: order=foo;bar' instead.
bootdisk.name: bootdisk

bwlimit.default: restore limit from datacenter or storage config
bwlimit.name: bwlimit

cdrom.description: This is an alias for option -ide2
cdrom.name: cdrom

cicustom.description: cloud-init: Specify custom files to replace the automatically generated ones at start.
cicustom.name: cicustom

cipassword.description: cloud-init: Password to assign the user. Using this is generally not recommended. Use ssh keys instead. Also note that older cloud-init versions do not support hashed passwords.
cipassword.name: cipassword

citype.description: Specifies the cloud-init configuration format. The default depends on the configured operating system type (`ostype`. We use the `nocloud` format for Linux, and `configdrive2` for windows.
citype.name: citype

ciupgrade.description: cloud-init: do an automatic package upgrade after the first boot.
ciupgrade.name: ciupgrade

ciuser.description: cloud-init: User name to change ssh keys and password for instead of the image's configured default user.
ciuser.name: ciuser

cores.description: The number of cores per socket.
cores.name: cores

cpulimit.name: cpulimit
cpulimit.verbose_description:
Limit of CPU usage.

NOTE: If the computer has 2 CPUs, it has total of '2' CPU time. Value '0' indicates no CPU limit.

cpuunits.description: CPU weight for a VM, will be clamped to [1, 10000] in cgroup v2.
cpuunits.name: cpuunits
cpuunits.verbose_description: CPU weight for a VM. Argument is used in the kernel fair scheduler. The larger the number is, the more CPU time this VM gets. Number is relative to weights of all the other running VMs.

description.description: Description for the VM. Shown in the web-interface VM's summary. This is saved as comment inside the configuration file.
description.name: description

efidisk0.description: Configure a disk for storing EFI vars. Use the special syntax STORAGE_ID:SIZE_IN_GiB to allocate a new volume. Note that SIZE_IN_GiB is ignored here and that the default EFI vars are copied to the volume instead. Use STORAGE_ID:0 and the 'import-from' parameter to import from an existing volume.

efitype.description: Size and type of the OVMF EFI vars. '4m' is newer and recommended, and required for Secure Boot. For backwards compatibility, '2m' is used if not otherwise specified. Ignored for VMs with arch=aarch64 (ARM).

format.description: The drive's backing file's data format.

import-from.description: Create a new disk, importing from this source (volume ID or absolute path). When an absolute path is specified, it's up to you to ensure that the source is not actively used by another process during the import!

pre-enrolled-keys.description: Use am EFI vars template with distribution-specific and Microsoft Standard keys enrolled, if used with 'efitype=4m'. Note that this will enable Secure Boot by default, though it can still be turned off from within the VM.

size.description: Disk size. This is purely informational and has no effect.

efidisk0.name: efidisk0

force.description: Allow to overwrite existing VM.
force.name: force

freeze.description: Freeze CPU at startup (use 'c' monitor command to start execution).
freeze.name: freeze

hookscript.description: Script that will be executed during various steps in the vms lifetime.
hookscript.name: hookscript

hostpci[n].description: Map host PCI devices into guest.
hostpci[n].name: hostpci[n]
hostpci[n].verbose_description:
Map host PCI devices into guest.

NOTE: This option allows direct access to host hardware. So it is no longer
possible to migrate such machines - use with special care.

CAUTION: Experimental! User reported problems with this option.

hotplug.description: Selectively enable hotplug features. This is a comma separated list of hotplug features: 'network', 'disk', 'cpu', 'memory', 'usb' and 'cloudinit'. Use '0' to disable hotplug completely. Using '1' as value is an alias for the default `network,disk,usb`. USB hotplugging is possible for guests with machine version >= 7.1 and ostype l26 or windows > 7.
hotplug.name: hotplug

ide[n].description: Use volume as IDE hard disk or CD-ROM (n is 0 to 3). Use the special syntax STORAGE_ID:SIZE_IN_GiB to allocate a new volume. Use STORAGE_ID:0 and the 'import-from' parameter to import from an existing volume.

backup.description: Whether the drive should be included when making backups.

bps.description: Maximum r/w speed in bytes per second.

bps_max_length.description: Maximum length of I/O bursts in seconds.

bps_rd.description: Maximum read speed in bytes per second.

bps_rd_max_length.description: Maximum length of read I/O bursts in seconds.

bps_wr.description: Maximum write speed in bytes per second.

bps_wr_max_length.description: Maximum length of write I/O bursts in seconds.

cyls.description: Force the drive's physical geometry to have a specific cylinder count.

detect_zeroes.description: Controls whether to detect and try to optimize writes of zeroes.

discard.description: Controls whether to pass discard/trim requests to the underlying storage.

format.description: The drive's backing file's data format.

heads.description: Force the drive's physical geometry to have a specific head count.

import-from.description: Create a new disk, importing from this source (volume ID or absolute path). When an absolute path is specified, it's up to you to ensure that the source is not actively used by another process during the import!

iops.description: Maximum r/w I/O in operations per second.

iops_max.description: Maximum unthrottled r/w I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_max_length.description: Maximum length of I/O bursts in seconds.

iops_rd.description: Maximum read I/O in operations per second.

iops_rd_max.description: Maximum unthrottled read I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_rd_max_length.description: Maximum length of read I/O bursts in seconds.

iops_wr.description: Maximum write I/O in operations per second.

iops_wr_max.description: Maximum unthrottled write I/O pool in operations per second.

iops_wr_max_length.description: Maximum length of write I/O bursts in seconds.

mbps.description: Maximum r/w speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_max.description: Maximum unthrottled r/w pool in megabytes per second.

mbps_rd.description: Maximum read speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_rd_max.description: Maximum unthrottled read pool in megabytes per second.

mbps_wr.description: Maximum write speed in megabytes per second.

mbps_wr_max.description: Maximum unthrottled write pool in megabytes per second.

model.description: The drive's reported model name, url-encoded, up to 40 bytes long.

replicate.description: Whether the drive should considered for replication jobs.

secs.description: Force the drive's physical geometry to have a specific sector count.

serial.description: The drive's reported serial number, url-encoded, up to 20 bytes long.

shared.description: Mark this locally-managed volume as available on all nodes
shared.verbose_description:
Mark this locally-managed volume as available on all nodes.

WARNING: This option does not share the volume automatically, it assumes it is shared already!

size.description: Disk size. This is purely informational and has no effect.

snapshot.description: Controls qemu's snapshot mode feature. If activated, changes made to the disk are temporary and will be discarded when the VM is shutdown.

ssd.description: Whether to expose this drive as an SSD, rather than a rotational hard disk.

trans.description: Force disk geometry bios translation mode.

wwn.description: The drive's worldwide name, encoded as 16 bytes hex string, prefixed by '0x'.

ide[n].name: ide[n]

import-working-storage.description: A file-based storage with 'images' content-type enabled, which is used as an intermediary extraction storage during import. Defaults to the source storage.
import-working-storage.name: import-working-storage

ipconfig[n].description:
cloud-init: Specify IP addresses and gateways for the corresponding interface.

IP addresses use CIDR notation, gateways are optional but need an IP of the same type specified.

The special string 'dhcp' can be used for IP addresses to use DHCP, in which case no explicit
gateway should be provided.
For IPv6 the special string 'auto' can be used to use stateless autoconfiguration. This requires
cloud-init 19.4 or newer.

If cloud-init is enabled and neither an IPv4 nor an IPv6 address is specified, it defaults to using
dhcp on IPv4.
ipconfig[n].name: ipconfig[n]

ivshmem.description: Inter-VM shared memory. Useful for direct communication between VMs, or to the host.

name.description: The name of the file. Will be prefixed with 'pve-shm-'. Default is the VMID. Will be deleted when the VM is stopped.

size.description: The size of the file in MB.

ivshmem.name: ivshmem

keephugepages.description: Use together with hugepages. If enabled, hugepages will not not be deleted after VM shutdown and can be used for subsequent starts.
keephugepages.name: keephugepages

keyboard.description: Keyboard layout for VNC server. This option is generally not required and is often better handled from within the guest OS.
keyboard.name: keyboard

live-restore.description: Start the VM immediately while importing or restoring in the background.
live-restore.name: live-restore

localtime.description: Set the real time clock (RTC) to local time. This is enabled by default if the `ostype` indicates a Microsoft Windows OS.
localtime.name: localtime

viommu.description: Enable and set guest vIOMMU variant (Intel vIOMMU needs q35 to be set as machine type).

current.description: Current amount of online RAM for the VM in MiB. This is the maximum available memory when you use the balloon device.

migrate_downtime.description: Set maximum tolerated downtime (in seconds) for migrations. Should the migration not be able to converge in the very end, because too much newly dirtied RAM needs to be transferred, the limit will be increased automatically step-by-step until migration can converge.
migrate_downtime.name: migrate_downtime

migrate_speed.description: Set maximum speed (in MB/s) for migrations. Value 0 is no limit.
migrate_speed.name: migrate_speed

name.description: Set a name for the VM. Only used on the configuration web interface.
name.name: name

nameserver.description: cloud-init: Sets DNS server IP address for a container. Create will automatically use the setting from the host if neither searchdomain nor nameserver are set.
nameserver.name: nameserver

bridge.description:
Bridge to attach the network device to. The Proxmox VE standard bridge
is called 'vmbr0'.

If you do not specify a bridge, we create a kvm user (NATed) network
device, which provides DHCP and DNS services. The following addresses
are used:

 10.0.2.2   Gateway
 10.0.2.3   DNS Server
 10.0.2.4   SMB Server

The DHCP server assign addresses to the guest starting from 10.0.2.15.

firewall.description: Whether this interface should be protected by the firewall.

link_down.description: Whether this interface should be disconnected (like pulling the plug).

macaddr.description: MAC address. That address must be unique within your network. This is automatically generated if not specified.
macaddr.verbose_description: A common MAC address with the I/G (Individual/Group) bit not set.

model.description: Network Card Model. The 'virtio' model provides the best performance with very low CPU overhead. If your guest does not support this driver, it is usually best to use 'e1000'.

mtu.description: Force MTU, for VirtIO only. Set to '1' to use the bridge MTU

queues.description: Number of packet queues to be used on the device.

rate.description: Rate limit in mbps (megabytes per second) as floating point number.

tag.description: VLAN tag to apply to packets on this interface.

trunks.description: VLAN trunks to pass through this interface.

memory.description: Amount of memory this NUMA node provides.

onboot.description: Specifies whether a VM will be started during system bootup.
onboot.name: onboot

ostype.name: ostype
ostype.verbose_description:
Specify guest operating system. This is used to enable special
optimization/features for specific operating systems:

[horizontal]
other;; unspecified OS
wxp;; Microsoft Windows XP
w2k;; Microsoft Windows 2000
w2k3;; Microsoft Windows 2003
w2k8;; Microsoft Windows 2008
wvista;; Microsoft Windows Vista
win7;; Microsoft Windows 7
win8;; Microsoft Windows 8/2012/2012r2
win10;; Microsoft Windows 10/2016/2019
win11;; Microsoft Windows 11/2022/2025
l24;; Linux 2.4 Kernel
l26;; Linux 2.6 - 6.X Kernel
solaris;; Solaris/OpenSolaris/OpenIndiania kernel

parallel[n].description: Map host parallel devices (n is 0 to 2).
parallel[n].name: parallel[n]
parallel[n].verbose_description:
Map host parallel devices (n is 0 to 2).

NOTE: This option allows direct access to host hardware. So it is no longer possible to migrate such
machines - use with special care.

CAUTION: Experimental! User reported problems with this option.

pool.description: Add the VM to the specified pool.
pool.name: pool

protection.description: Sets the protection flag of the VM. This will disable the remove VM and remove disk operations.
protection.name: protection

reboot.description: Allow reboot. If set to '0' the VM exit on reboot.
reboot.name: reboot

rng0.description: Configure a VirtIO-based Random Number Generator.

max_bytes.description: Maximum bytes of entropy allowed to get injected into the guest every 'period' milliseconds. Prefer a lower value when using '/dev/random' as source. Use `0` to disable limiting (potentially dangerous!).

period.description: Every 'period' milliseconds the entropy-injection quota is reset, allowing the guest to retrieve another 'max_bytes' of entropy.

source.description: The file on the host to gather entropy from. In most cases '/dev/urandom' should be preferred over '/dev/random' to avoid entropy-starvation issues on the host. Using urandom does *not* decrease security in any meaningful way, as it's still seeded from real entropy, and the bytes provided will most likely be mixed with real entropy on the guest as well. '/dev/hwrng' can be used to pass through a hardware RNG from the host.

rng0.name: rng0

sata[n].description: Use volume as SATA hard disk or CD-ROM (n is 0 to 5). Use the special syntax STORAGE_ID:SIZE_IN_GiB to allocate a new volume. Use STORAGE_ID:0 and the 'import-from' parameter to import from an existing volume.
